svg.text: yata — Rust Cypher Graph Engine Architecture
14 crates | 985+ tests | CSR + Arrow IPC WAL + Per-label Compaction + R2 | Critical Path + Shannon + Kaizen + Security

g.text: Deploy new image → Container restart → CSR lost
→ R2 page-in 12-52s → ALL queries return empty
Fix: edge cache restored + write fallback
P1: Read Replicas Empty After Deploy
WAL push = fire-and-forget (no ack, no retry)
Cold start = cron 5min (not on first query)
PDS cannot distinguish empty vs no-data
cyCached → cy() direct (every request to yata)
Before: CF Cache API 60s TTL masked cold starts
After: zero resilience to yata unavailability
Result: feed/search empty during cold start
A: Current (CSR+WAL+edge cache) η=71.8% Avail=95% Cold=12-52s Cost=$3/mo
B: R2 Direct Query (no Container) η=58.2% Avail=99.9% Cold=0s Cost=$0.50/mo
C: Warm Standby (blue-green) η=71.8% Avail=99.5% Cold=0s Cost=$6/mo
D: Hybrid R2+CSR (recommended) η=68.4% Avail=99.9% Cold=0s Cost=$3/mo ← R2 scan fallback when CSR cold
E: Pipeline Materialized View η=75.1% Avail=99.9% Cold=0s Cost=$0.50/mo (Pipeline GA required)
deps: grin, cypher, format, core, memmap2
deps: engine, graph, s3, object, axum, tokio, metrics
1. App -> PDS_RPC.createRecord -> Pipeline.send() (durable) -> YATA_RPC.mergeRecord() -> WAL append + dirty_labels.insert(label)
2. Compaction cron: wal_flush_segment() -> drain_dirty_labels() -> per dirty label: PK-dedup -> R2 PUT label/{label}.arrow
3. Clean labels = zero R2 I/O | Manifest v2 per-label tracking | v1 monolithic auto-migration
Shannon Information-Theoretic Analysis — Before vs After
P2: trigger_compaction() read ALL segments + full compacted segment → re-compact ALL
O(total_entries) per cycle. dirty_labels tracked but never drained (leak)
P9: monolithic compacted segment grew unbounded with total state
1 label change → re-read + re-write entire compacted segment (all labels)
P4: Cold start full page-in (already mitigated: label-selective + topology-only)
P5: Coordinator 2ms fan-out overhead (mitigated: adaptive sqrt(N))
P6: Dual persistence — Pipeline WAL + R2 snapshot (by design, cold start <5s)
Clean labels = zero R2 I/O (no read, no write)
Shannon Efficiency (N=1M vertices, delta=100 per snapshot)
O(dirty) filter + dedup dirty labels only
Delta PUT reduces R2 I/O cost
14 crates | ~44,500 LOC | 1,068 tests pass | Edition 2024 | Apache-2.0 | cargo zigbuild x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
Latency breakdown per operation | Bottleneck nodes in red | Production E2E measured values
CP4: Compaction Path (Per-label delta — O(dirty) not O(total))
Incremental delta for small mutations. Full rebuild fallback for delta>50%
Container-side batch: N statements in 1 HTTP round-trip
PDS Worker + YataRPC Coordinator + yata Container + XRPC Transport | 7/10 FIXED (S1,S3,S4,S5 + K1-K5)
S1: commit() 4/6 rebuild now dirty-aware — FIXED (zero-copy for clean labels)
rebuild_prop_indexes + rebuild_columnar_cache + rebuild_label_bitmap + rebuild_btree_indexes skip clean labels
S2: L1 Arrow IPC WAL — unified format (zero-copy mmap, segment registry, L1 compaction)
Shannon: L0 Pipeline.send() = source of truth. L1 = safety net while Pipeline Beta. Post-GA: delete L1
S3: ensure_labels() now uses label hints — FIXED (on-demand enrich_label_from_r2)
Hot path: loaded_labels tracking + per-label R2 GET via enrich_label_from_r2(). Cold start: full page-in preserved.
eta 5% → 85%. 100 labels, 2 label query: 100 blob → 2 blob R2 I/O. Dead code activated.
S4: YataRPC regex per-call compilation — FIXED (cached as static fields)
container/yata/src/index.ts:160,256,234 | LABEL_RE, MUTATION_RE, AGG_RE now static readonly + Set dedup
Fix: -50-100us/query. extractLabelHints uses Set instead of Array.includes O(N^2)
wal.ts sendWalAndProject: Pipeline.send + doMergeWithRetry now run via Promise.all (independent paths)
Write latency = max(Pipeline, mergeRecord) instead of sum. Double serialization remains (by design).
CF Container = gVisor sandbox. Workers RPC zero-copy impossible.
HTTP ~1-5ms per hop. Design: CSR <1us absorbs. Not fixable.
index.ts:242 | .catch(warn) only. No retry, no DLQ.
~1us overhead. Could merge timeout into BindingTransport
Needs measurement before tuning. Not Shannon waste.
4/6 rebuild dirty-aware: prop_indexes, columnar_cache, label_bitmap, btree_indexes skip clean labels
Label hints wired to enrich_label_from_r2. loaded_labels tracking. Dead code activated.
eta 5% → 85%. Hot path: per-label R2 GET only. 999 tests pass.
Cache regex as static readonly. extractLabelHints Set dedup. mergeResults cached AGG_RE
Container /xrpc/ai.gftd.yata.cypherBatch: N statements in 1 HTTP. cypherBatchRead: per-partition grouping + auth
Cypher mutation: delta<50% → add/delete vertices+edges on existing CSR O(delta). Fallback: full rebuild O(V+E)
last_snapshot_serialize_ms in CpmStats. Auto-warn at vertex>100K or serialize>100ms with projected latency
Early detection → parallel serialize when needed
Delete walFlushSegment cron + pushWalToReplicas once Pipeline exits Beta
Promise.all([walStream.send, doMergeWithRetry]) — Pipeline + mergeRecord in parallel
Add exponential backoff retry (3x) with DLQ. Reduces silent WAL entry loss
Critical Chain Dependencies (updated 2026-03-29 — 4 major fixes applied)
CP2 (Cold) FIXED (S3/K2): ensure_labels now uses label hints → enrich_label_from_r2 per-label R2 GET. Hot path: loaded_labels skip. eta 5% → 85%.
CP5 (Mutation) FIXED (S1/K1): commit() 4/6 rebuild dirty-aware. rebuild_columnar_cache/label_bitmap/prop_indexes/btree skip clean labels. 200MB → 20MB.
CP1 (Write) FIXED (S5/K5): Pipeline.send + mergeRecord now Promise.all (parallel). Write latency ~40ms → ~25ms. mergeRecord failure still recoverable via WAL.
R7 (Snapshot) FIXED: dirty AtomicBool removed. dirty_labels is SSoT. Edge/topology blobs always uploaded when dirty_set non-empty.
Full-Stack Write Path — Shannon Redundancy Visualization
PK-dedup Arrow IPC — legacy ArrowFragment 除去済み
L1 Arrow IPC WAL — unified format (mmap-ready)
WAL: Arrow IPC (mmap-ready, was NDJSON text)
Primary (solid green) vs Fallback (dashed amber/red) | Trigger + latency delta
CSR stale. Pipeline WAL durable. Recovery: gftd wal-replay
FB1c: Pipeline reject = NOT durable. Client must retry.
FB4c: R2 PUT error next cron. WAL backup.
Pipeline.send() resolve = durable. All other state (CSR, snapshots, replicas) is ephemeral, rebuildable from Pipeline WAL. gftd wal-replay = universal recovery.
Full stack: App Worker + PDS Worker + YataRPC Coordinator + yata Container (Write + Read x N) + R2 + XRPC Transport
3-Layer Auth | GIE SecurityFilter O(1)/vertex | DID-native Identity | Clearance × RBAC × Consent
L1: Service Binding Trust (Workers RPC internal)
L2: ES256 Service Auth JWT (DID-signed P-256 ECDSA)
ring::signature P-256 verify | DID Document pubkey from CSR
Phishing-resistant | Registration attestation verified by auth Worker
Cache: 5min TTL, invalidated on ClearanceAssignment/ConsentGrant mutation
GIE SecurityFilter — Inline O(1) per Vertex (No MemoryGraph Fallback)
LogicalOp::SecurityFilter injected after each Scan/Expand op
DID format: did:web:auth.gftd.ai:{controller}/{path} | P-256 ECDSA | z-prefixed base58btc multibase
End-to-end: XRPC request → JWT verify → SecurityScope compile → GIE transpile_secured → filtered results
Cypher node type, Arrow IPC blob unit
ES256 P-256 sig verify + DID pubkey from CSR + exp ±30s
GIE SecurityFilter + owner_hash + sensitivity_ord per vertex
FNV-1a non-invertible + DID binding in ConsentGrant
Parameterized queries (esc()), no raw string interpolation
Mitigated by T2 agent self-custody + T3 human self-custody
TLS: rustls (ring backend) — ureq sync S3
Key Enc: z-prefix base58btc multibase (P-256 65B uncompressed)
3-layer auth | O(1)/vertex GIE filter | DID-native identity (did:web) | T1/T2/T3 key custody | ES256 P-256 ECDSA | 4-level clearance | RBAC + consent
DID Document Resolution (CSR-native, no HTTPS fallback for internal DIDs)
:DIDDocument {did, controller_did, public_key_multibase, performer_type, key_custody_tier, created_at, updated_at}
resolve_did_pubkey(did) → CSR query → z-prefixed base58btc → decompress P-256 (33B→65B) → ring::UnparsedPublicKey
1. DID = canonical identity (no ephemeral IDs, no Clerk orgs) 2. ES256 > HS256 for service-to-service 3. yata CSR = SecurityScope source of truth
4. SecurityFilter is O(1) (no MemoryGraph fallback) 5. Partition ≠ security boundary 6. Consent via owner_hash (FNV-1a O(1)) 7. AT Protocol faithful (Session+ServiceAuth JWT)
Hasse diagram: edge = direct dependency (transitive omitted). Meet-semilattice: ⊥ = yata-core ∧ yata-grin. Shannon: each crate = unique information, no redundant node.
Interior: arrow, format, cypher, store, gie, s3+obj, engine+graph+vex
Meet-semilattice: ∀ a,b ∈ Crates, a ∧ b = greatest common dependency. Acyclic (Cargo enforces). Unique ⊥ = {core, grin}. Unique ⊤ = {server}.
Shannon criterion: each node contributes unique information (H > 0). ⚠ nodes have H ≈ 0 (single-consumer wrapper = entropy redundancy). Merge restores H > 0.
